channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Ravenstorm At The Center Of Airbus’s New Combat Drone Portfolio | The War Zone
item.description: As Europe’s flagship next-generation fighter effort unravels, Airbus is betting heavily on uncrewed collaborative combat aircraft. The post Ravenstorm At The Center Of Airbus’s New Combat Drone Portfolio appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 AH-64 Apache Shot Down By Iran, U.S. Will Retaliate: Trump | The War Zone
item.description: The AH-64 crashed into the Gulf of Oman and its crew was rescued by a drone boat, and now Trump says it was due to enemy action. The post AH-64 Apache Shot Down By Iran, U.S. Will Retaliate: Trump appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 How Can Lebanon’s Partners Help Strengthen the Lebanese Armed Forces? | War on the Rocks
item.description: The Lebanese state’s lack of effective sovereignty is a crucial problem in efforts to negotiate with Israel and disarm Hizballah. In response to intensified fighting between Israel and Hizballah, the United States began hosting talks between Israel and the Lebanese government in…

item.title: 🕵️ NRO funds BlackSky for new satellites, AI-optimized image detection system | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The NRO is at the forefront of integrating AI into space-based ISR,” a spysat agency spokesperson told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🏴 Four questions (and expert answers) about Armenia’s elections and what to expect next | Atlantic Council
item.description: Atlantic Council experts look at what Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan’s victory means for the South Caucasus region and for the US and Russia. The post Four questions (and expert answers) about Armenia’s elections and what to expect next appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Naval carrier set to deploy with a drone, while Marines say goodbye to the Harrier | Breaking Defense
item.description: This week on The Break Out, a Seahawk drone is set to join aircraft carrier Theodore Roosevelt, and a legendary aircraft took its final flight.

item.title: 🏛️ After FCAS demise, Germany’s options include ordering more F-35 warplanes | Defense News
item.description: “Knowing what we know today, we wouldn’t set up a program in this way again,” Pistorius said.

item.title: 🌍 Lebanon Is No Longer About Lebanon | Foreign Policy
item.description: Iran would rather fight than let Israel decide Lebanon’s fate.

item.title: 📍 Breaking Defense adds Kyle Rempfer as additional editor, amid continued growth | Breaking Defense
item.description: Kyle Rempfer to join the world’s largest news organization dedicated to defense.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon reveals preferred munitions for one-way attack drones | Defense News
item.description: The list includes Northrop Grumman and several startups competing to supply low-cost payloads compatible with any drone design.

item.title: 📍 AH-64 Apache Crew Rescued By Drone Boat After Going Down Near Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: This is the first known use of a uncrewed surface vessel to recover downed aircrew and is major sign of what's to come. The post AH-64 Apache Crew Rescued By Drone Boat After Going Down Near Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Protecting Environmental Rights Defenders Is Key to Giving Communities a Voice | Just Security
item.description: Environmental human rights defenders must be empowered to design and implement their own forms of collective protection to shift the power imbalance. The post Protecting Environmental Rights Defenders Is Key to Giving Communities a Voice appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Putin offers Su-57 to India as New Delhi faces stealth fighter gap | Defense News
item.description: The co-production and customization being offered by Russia marks one of the most expansive defense cooperation proposals made in recent years to India.

item.title: 🏛️ US soldiers rescued by drone after Apache helicopter goes down near the coast of Oman | Defense News
item.description: U.S. Naval Forces Central Command's Task Force 59 and the Army's 82nd Airborne Division led the rescue, with Air Force assets assisting, as well.

item.title: 📍 Beyond Tech-Facilitated Gender-Based Violence: The Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women, Gender, and the Governance of Digital Economies in ASEAN | Just Security
item.description: The Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women is quietly becoming a normative force in the governance of digital economies. The post Beyond Tech-Facilitated Gender-Based Violence: The Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women, Gender, and…

item.title: 📍 The New October 7 Tribunal and the Legitimacy Challenge of Atrocity Adjudication | Just Security
item.description: The tribunal will be judged not only by the verdicts it produces, but by the institutional model of accountability it leaves behind. The post The New October 7 Tribunal and the Legitimacy Challenge of Atrocity Adjudication appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Drones Over Europe: The Prohibition on the Use of Force and Unintended Harm | Just Security
item.description: Russia’s drone incursions into E.U. territory raise important questions about how unintended engagements are regulated under international law. The post Drones Over Europe: The Prohibition on the Use of Force and Unintended Harm appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian civilians face new threat from Russia’s upgraded jet drones | Atlantic Council
item.description: Russia has reportedly begun deploying a new generation of jet-powered strike drones against Ukrainian targets in recent weeks as the Kremlin seeks to counter the growing effectiveness of Ukraine’s interceptor drones, writes David Kirichenko. The post Ukrainian civilians face new…

item.title: 🌏 Xi and Kim Agree to Strengthen ‘Strategic Relationship’ with Socialist Principles | The Diplomat
item.description: Touting the achievements Pyongyang has made in recent years, Xi de facto recognized North Korea as a nuclear-armed state.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 9, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated guide to major news and developments over the weekend. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR A U.S. Army helicopter went down near the coast of Oman on Monday night but President Donald Trump said the pilots were “fine.”…

item.title: 📍 How Kazakhstan Uses the Stalling Board of Peace | The Diplomat
item.description: Despite opting out of contributing the $1 billion fee required to become a permanent member of Donald Trump's Board of Peace, Kazakhstan has continued to publicly highlight its membership.

item.title: 📍 Airbus-led alliance lobbies Germany on fighter jet project | Defense News
item.description: Industry sources said the move did not amount to an attempt to launch a new fighter project following the collapse of the Franco-German FCAS program.

item.title: 📍 Atlantic Council marks the Tenth Atlantic Council Global Energy Forum | Atlantic Council
item.description: Featuring a keynote conversation with US Energy Secretary Christopher Wright WASHINGTON, DC – JUNE 9, 2026 – The 10th Atlantic Council Global Energy Forum will take place on June 9th and 10th in Washington, DC. The Atlantic Council’s Global Energy Center marks the Forum’s…

item.title: 🏴 Countries across Russia’s former empire are reclaiming place names to assert identity | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine's campaign to strip Soviet and Russian imperial place names from the country’s towns and cities is not a reaction to the current war so much as the leading edge of a region-wide rejection of Moscow's cartography, writes Joseph Epstein. The post Countries across Russia’s…

item.title: 🕵️ Cockroach Janta Party: Is the Modi Government Wary of a ‘Gen Z Revolt’? | The Diplomat
item.description: Hitherto confined to social media, the "cockroaches" emerged on the ground at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar on June 6. What started as a joke has turned serious.

item.title: 🌏 U.S. Power Is Wrung Out | Foreign Policy
item.description: The war in Iran has depleted the Pentagon as China’s military buildup matures.

item.title: 🏴 NATO allies need a plan for addressing threats below the threshold of war | Atlantic Council
item.description: NATO can gather allies to share best practices and also coordinate responses, even if the Alliance does not respond to subthreshold attacks itself. The post NATO allies need a plan for addressing threats below the threshold of war appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 After the fall of FCAS fighter, Germany eyes ‘realistic’ future projects with France | Breaking Defense
item.description: After years of squabbles over the development of the sixth-gen fighter jet, the centerpiece of the projected $115 billion effort, Berlin said the companies involved “cannot reach an agreement.”

item.title: 📍 How to Escape Blame for Bad Advice | Foreign Policy
item.description: If you get an important strategic issue wrong, there are still ways to avoid accountability.

item.title: 📍 Is VinFast’s Costly EV Gambit Coming to an End? | The Diplomat
item.description: Despite its vaunting ambitions and rapid expansion, the upstart automaker has yet to prove it can compete beyond the Vietnamese market.
